title: 拆警翼行動第五波：澳紐共約一萬三千間中小學！！！

replies.msg:
請廣傳！

拆警翼行動第五波：澳紐共約一萬三千間中小學

此行動旨在響應黑社會大佬何妖嘅呼籲，鼓勵恐怖份子嘅仔女去大灣區讀書，唔好去英美加澳勾結外國勢力。我借用陳淑莊響聯合國嘅演詞，寫咗電郵，勸澳紐中小學小心處理垃圾狗仔狗女嘅入學申請。

請大家幫忙花啲時間發電郵俾澳紐呢啲學校，一萬三千個地址，請隨便揀啲嚟寄。

下一波預告：澳紐約五十間大學

第一波回顧：英國教育官員
https://lihkg.com/thread/1596786/page/1
第二波回顧：英國共二萬多間中小學
https://lihkg.com/thread/1636110/page/1
第三波回顧：英國共一百多間大學
https://lihkg.com/thread/1652131/page/1
第四波回顧：澳紐教育官員
https://lihkg.com/thread/1744265/page/1

Subject: IMPORTANT: Caution Against Applications from Hong Kong Students

Dear Sir/Madam:

I am writing to suggest you exercising extreme caution in reviewing admission applications from Hong Kong students, with respect to whether their parents are police officers, which should be carefully verified and considered in the assessment.

Hong Kong is on the verge of a humanitarian crisis, as police brutality against supporters of the democratic movement escalates.

Hong Kong police brutality database: http://tl.hkrev.info/en/police-timeline/

Arrested protesters face physical abuse and humiliation by the police. A few female protesters openly spoke of sexual abuse including rape by the police. More cases remain unreported. Detained protesters were denied timely access to medical and legal assistance.

Use of force is excessive and indiscriminate. High school students were shot by live police rounds at close range and was denied for emergency treatment. Beanbag shots and plastic pellets by the police had blinded the eyes first-aiders and foreign journalists. The riot police attacked innocent subway passengers whom they claimed were rioters in disguise. As of now the police have fired more than 10,000 tear gas cans, many of which had expired and might have turned chemically dangerous to human health.

The police call protesters “cockroaches”. Brutal crackdowns and pre-emptive violence against them are hence regarded as acceptable pest control to curb free speech.

There are now international efforts, such as the Hong Kong Human Rights and Democracy Act of 2019 recently passed in the United States, trying to sanction Hong Kong police and their families, and classify them as terrorists. Australia is considering a law similar to that of the United States’ Magnitsky Act that would sanction human rights violators. If justice does exist in our world, Hong Kong police will be eventually prosecuted by legal authorities including the International Crime Court for crimes against humanity.

Having committed war crimes, the police are worried about the safety of their children, who are now carrying personal alarms all the time, inside and outside schools. They are urgently sending their children to schools oversea as reported by Chinese-language newspapers Apple Daily on September 23:

https://hk.news.appledaily.com/local/daily/article/20190923/20773403

As a result, in reviewing applications from Hong Kong students with police parents, you may want to consider whether the applicants appreciate liberty and justice, whether they will make conflicts with their classmates, whether they will harm your school reputation, and whether they will be able to pay school fees as their parents will be jailed. The names of some senior police officers can be found in government telephone directory:

https://www.directory.gov.hk/basic_search.jsp?lang=eng

Thank you very much for your kind attention.

Yours faithfully,

One of the many Hong Kong citizens under the threat of life from the police
